records.display_name: Community issues: long and slow trips, underserved areas

payload.claim_text: Key issues raised at public workshops included: Bus trips are long and slow; Many parts of South Brooklyn are underserved by transit; A bus trip across South Brooklyn can take up to 2 hours
payload.description: Key issues raised at public workshops for the South Brooklyn corridor.

payload.claim_text: Delays at particular intersections/bottlenecks; Parking/double parking/delivery conflicts
payload.description: Identified traffic concerns on the B82 corridor

payload.claim_text: High crash locations: Flatlands & Ralph, Flatlands & Rockaway Pkwy, Kings Hwy & Nostrand, Kings Hwy & Ocean Pkwy, Bay Pkwy & 86th St
payload.description: High crash locations identified as safety concerns

payload.claim_text: Long delays at bus stops, traffic signals; Traffic conflicts; Bus stops, access to bus stops need improvement; Crowding during rush hours & before/after school
payload.description: Identified transit concerns on the B82 corridor

payload.claim_text: #1 More reliable/on-time service; Articulated buses; Faster service; More & nicer bus shelters & benches at stops; More frequent night service; Extend B82 to Gateway Center Mall
payload.description: What we've heard on the street - top community feedback items

records.display_name: BRT Phase II Study identified South Brooklyn corridor as priority

payload.claim_text: The Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study (2009) identified the South Brooklyn east-west corridor as a priority service need.
payload.description: The 2009 BRT Phase II Study identified the South Brooklyn east-west corridor as a priority service need.

payload.description: All-door boarding allowing buses to spend less time waiting at bus stops.
